abstract: The protocol for exporting Objective-C objects to JavaScript.
overview:
## Overview

Implement this protocol to export your Objective-C classes and their instance methods, class methods, and properties to JavaScript code.

### Export Objective-C Objects to JavaScript

When you create a JavaScript value from an instance of an Objective-C class, and the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue] class doesn’t specify a copying convention, JavaScriptCore creates a JavaScript wrapper object. For certain classes, JavaScriptCore automatically copies values to the appropriate JavaScript type; for example, [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/Foundation/NSString] instances become JavaScript strings.

JavaScript supports inheritance using a chain of prototype objects. For each Objective-C class you export, JavaScriptCore creates a prototype within the enclosing JavaScript context (a [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSContext] object). For the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/ObjectiveC/NSObject-swift.class] class, the prototype object is the JavaScript context’s `Object` prototype. For all other Objective-C classes, JavaScriptCore creates a prototype object with an internal `[Prototype]` property that points to the prototype property it creates for the Objective-C class’s superclass. As such, the prototype chain for a JavaScript wrapper object reflects the wrapped Objective-C type’s inheritance hierarchy.

In addition to the prototype object, JavaScriptCore produces a JavaScript constructor object for each Objective-C class.

### Expose Objective-C Methods and Properties to JavaScript

By default, no methods or properties of the Objective-C class have exposure to JavaScript. Instead, you must choose methods and properties to export. For each protocol that a class conforms to, if the protocol incorporates the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSExport] protocol, JavaScriptCore interprets that protocol as a list of methods and properties to export to JavaScript.

For each instance method you export, JavaScriptCore creates a corresponding JavaScript function as a property of the prototype object. For each Objective-C property you export, JavaScriptCore creates a JavaScript accessor property on the prototype. For each class method you export, JavaScriptCore creates a JavaScript function on the constructor object. The following code example illustrates adoption of the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSExport] protocol:

The following code example illustrates the API that an exported class presents in JavaScript:

The attributes of an Objective-C `@property` declaration determine the attributes of the corresponding JavaScript property as follows:

Wrapped Objective-C properties, parameters, and return values convert according to the copying convention that the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue] class specifies for their types. See [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSValue] for the complete list of copying conventions.

### Customize Export of Objective-C Selectors

When exporting a selector that takes one or more arguments, JavaScriptCore generates a corresponding function name using the following conversion:

For example, under the default conversion, the Objective-C selector `doX:withY:` exports as the JavaScript function `doXWithY`.

To rename a selector that you export to JavaScript, use the `JSExportAs` macro. For example, to instead export the Objective-C selector `doX:withY:` as the JavaScript function `doX`, use the following declaration:
title: JSExport
